qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Command injection in ExecuteCommand node permits arbitrary OS command execution
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The advisory describes a Python Code node sandbox escape vulnerability where insufficient restriction of built-in Python objects allows file exfiltration or RCE. This finding describes a command injection vulnerability in the ExecuteCommand node, which is a completely different node type, vulnerability class, and root cause. The advisory root cause is missing Python sandbox restrictions, while this finding's root cause is unrestricted exec() calls in the ExecuteCommand node.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: SSRF in workflow import from URL allows internal network access and metadata theft
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The advisory describes a Python Code node sandbox escape vulnerability. This finding describes an SSRF vulnerability in the workflow import from URL feature, which is a different vulnerable component (workflows.controller.ts vs Python Code node), different attack vector (workflow import URL parameter vs Python code execution), and different root cause (missing URL validation vs insufficient Python sandbox restrictions).

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: XXE injection in XML parsing allows file disclosure and SSRF
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The advisory describes a Python Code node sandbox escape vulnerability. This finding describes an XXE injection vulnerability in XML parsing using xml2js, which is a different vulnerable component (XML parser vs Python Code node), different attack vector (malicious XML payloads vs Python code execution), and different root cause (missing XXE protections in xml2js configuration vs insufficient Python sandbox restrictions).

target_advisory_judgement.reason: None of the three qualifying vulnerabilities identify the advisory-described Python Code node sandbox escape vulnerability. The advisory describes insufficient restrictions on built-in Python objects in the Python Code node that allow authenticated users to escape the sandbox and achieve file exfiltration or RCE. The discovered findings are: (1) command injection in ExecuteCommand node, (2) SSRF in workflow import from URL, and (3) XXE injection in XML parsing. All three are different vulnerability types in different components with different root causes than the advisory-described vulnerability.
evidence: Advisory summary: 'n8n has Arbitrary File Read via Python Code Node Sandbox Escape'

Advisory details: 'An authenticated user with permission to create or modify workflows could use the Python Code node to escape the sandbox. The sandbox did not sufficiently restrict access to certain built-in Python objects'
Finding 1: ExecuteCommand node command injection - different node type, different vulnerability class
Finding 2: Workflow import SSRF - different feature, different vulnerability class
Finding 3: XML parsing XXE - different parser, different vulnerability class
No finding references Python Code node, Python sandbox restrictions, or built-in Python object access
